key_provisions: Generative AI cannot be designated as an author or peer reviewer
Data privacy review required before entering protected (HIPAA/FERPA) data into AI tools
Faculty must establish course-level AI policies; CELT provides example policies and Student AI Use Scale
Disclosure of AI use required in research/publications; compliance with funder and journal rules
AI detector scores treated with skepticism due to reliability/bias concerns
Separate clinical guidelines address multimodal AI in patient-care settings
ai_policy.summary: UK's AI policy framework is maintained by the transdisciplinary UK ADVANCE Team (transitioning into the CATS AI Guidelines for Operations Group), which published instructional, research, clinical, and professional guidelines beginning April 2024 with revisions in April 2025. The guidelines cover faculty, students, staff, research, and clinical care, emphasizing responsible use, data protection, and transparent disclosure rather than outright prohibition.

governance_structure.committee_name: Commonwealth AI Transdisciplinary Strategy (CATS AI) / UK ADVANCE Team
governance_structure.notes: CATS AI, announced by President Capilouto on Nov 13, 2025, is UK's institution-wide AI governance and execution structure. Interim co-directors: Ian McClure (VP for Innovation) and Heath Price (AVP for Partnerships), with a permanent director search underway. Five subcommittees: AI Research (co-chairs Chad Risko, Brent Seales), AI Education (Hubie Ballard), AI Health (Romil Chadha), AI Administration (co-chairs Adam Recktenwald, Jake Stover), and AI for Students (Taylor Blair). The preceding UK ADVANCE Team (co-chaired by Provost Bob DiPaola and Assistant Provost Trey Conatser) continues to steward academic AI guidelines. Task force originally led by Provost DiPaola and EVPFA Eric Monday. Operates under the Advancing Kentucky Together (AKT) Network.

vendor_deployments.product: Microsoft 365 Copilot (incl. Copilot Studio, Agent Store, GitHub Copilot)
vendor_deployments.notes: Partnership announced Nov 25, 2025 as part of CATS AI; Microsoft joined the Advancing Kentucky Together (AKT) Network as a corporate partner. Copilot licenses opened to all students, faculty, and staff on Jan 29, 2026 via myUK portal. UK is described as 'one of the nation's first institutions' to partner with Microsoft on this framework. Enterprise-grade: UK data not used to train public models; meets UK's cybersecurity and privacy standards.
UK HealthCare deployment for physicians and nurses to improve clinical documentation and patient care.
Research enterprise deployment for scientific studies.

ai_academic_programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Artificial Intelligence

notable_deals_investments.title: UK-Microsoft Responsible AI Partnership (CATS AI)
notable_deals_investments.summary: Microsoft joined UK's Advancing Kentucky Together (AKT) Network as a corporate partner, providing campus-wide access to Copilot, Copilot Studio, M365, Agent Store, GitHub Copilot, Dragon Copilot (clinical), and the Discovery Platform (research). No dollar value disclosed.

leadership_statements.speaker: Eli Capilouto
leadership_statements.quote_or_summary: "UK must lead in this moment. We must appropriately use AI to enhance and accelerate achieving our commitment to the Commonwealth."

leadership_statements.speaker: Eli Capilouto
leadership_statements.quote_or_summary: "This collaboration reflects what's possible when innovation is guided by purpose. Microsoft is helping place Kentucky in the lead - not only in terms of early access to powerful tools but in how those tools are applied to solve real challenges with communities."

state_ai_policy_context.summary: Kentucky enacted SB 4 (signed by Gov. Andy Beshear on March 24, 2025) establishing a risk-based AI governance framework for state government. The law creates an AI Governance Committee within the Commonwealth Office of Technology, requires disclosure of AI use in decision-making, mandates human oversight for consequential decisions, and adds election-integrity provisions. It applies to state government agencies; application to public universities is indirect but sets a baseline compliance tone. Kentucky also stood up a legislative Special Committee / AI Task Force (2024 HCR 38). No separate statewide higher-education-system AI policy; UK (and each public institution) governs its own AI use.

narrative_summary: The University of Kentucky is one of the more advanced institution profiles on AI posture and a useful benchmark for the institution. Governance runs through the Commonwealth AI Transdisciplinary Strategy (CATS AI), launched by President Eli Capilouto in November 2025 with interim co-directors Ian McClure and Heath Price, a steering council, and five operating subcommittees (research, education, health, administration, students). Written policy has existed since April 2024 via the UK ADVANCE Team, which published faculty, research, clinical, and professional guidelines (revised April 2025) covering disclosure, authorship, data protection, and course-level policy expectations. On deployments, UK went campus-wide with Microsoft 365 Copilot on January 29, 2026, under a broader Microsoft partnership that also brings Dragon Copilot to UK HealthCare and the Discovery Platform to research - branded as one of the first such enterprise-scale, responsibly-framed deployments in the country. Academically, UK launched the state's first AI undergraduate certificate (Spring 2025) and the state's first BS in Artificial Intelligence (Fall 2026). State policy exposure is moderate: Kentucky SB 4 (March 2025) regulates state government AI use under a risk-based framework but does not directly bind public universities. There is no named dedicated AI leader yet - a permanent CATS AI director search is underway - but UK has a clearly dedicated AI leadership structure, vendor depth, and board-visible presidential sponsorship.
confidence_notes: HIGH confidence on governance, Microsoft deployment, state policy, and academic programs (primary uky.edu sources). MEDIUM confidence on exact policy adoption date (April 2024 per EdScoop; revised April 2025). Gaps: no disclosed dollar value for the Microsoft partnership; no specific Copilot license count; no evidence of ChatGPT Edu, Claude for Education, or Gemini enterprise deployments (Microsoft appears to be the sole enterprise AI partner); technology leader Brian Nichols confirmed via UK ITS but appointment predates current AI era (2016); no identified AI-related litigation or controversy.
